name: Law Office of Christopher Graham
description_short: New York bankruptcy and tax attorney handling Chapter 7/13 filings, foreclosure defense, and IRS/state tax resolution since 1998.
description_long:
The Law Office of Christopher Graham is a New York-based legal practice founded in 1998, specializing in bankruptcy law, tax resolution, and foreclosure defense. Led by Attorney Christopher Graham, who worked as an accountant for over a decade before practicing law, the firm brings a dual perspective to complex financial cases. The firm serves consumers throughout New York facing debt, foreclosure, or tax obligations.

The firm offers comprehensive legal services including Chapter 7 and Chapter 13 bankruptcy filings, foreclosure defense against lawsuits, IRS and state tax debt resolution (including taxpayer audits and offers-in-compromise), and specialized Chapter 13 bankruptcy strategies for tax solutions when non-bankruptcy alternatives are unavailable. They emphasize affordable representation and free initial case evaluations available by phone or in-person.

What distinguishes this firm is the attorney's background in accounting combined with legal training, enabling more sophisticated tax analysis alongside bankruptcy work. The firm has earned an AV® Preeminent Rating from Martindale-Hubbell®, indicating high ethical standards and legal ability. They position themselves on personal service and long-term financial solutions rather than quick fixes, with 20+ years of combined experience in tax, bankruptcy, and legal matters.

As a legal services provider, clients should understand that this is a solo or small firm operation rather than a large bankruptcy mill. While they emphasize affordability, actual costs will depend on case complexity. The free consultation allows prospective clients to assess fit before committing. Results in bankruptcy and tax cases depend heavily on individual circumstances and debtor cooperation.
address: 157 Columbus Ave 4th floor, New York, NY 10023

company_info.headquarters: 157 Columbus Ave 4th floor, New York, NY 10023

pros: Attorney holds AV® Preeminent Rating from Martindale-Hubbell®, indicating high professional standing
Founder has dual expertise: 10+ years as accountant plus legal training, enabling sophisticated tax analysis
Files both Chapter 7 and Chapter 13 bankruptcies routinely, indicating substantial bankruptcy practice experience
Offers foreclosure defense specifically, protecting against home loss in addition to debt relief
Negotiates directly with IRS and state tax authorities on behalf of clients
Operating continuously since 1998 (20+ years established), providing track record and stability
Free case evaluations available by phone or in-person, lowering barrier to legal advice
cons: Solo or small firm operation—limited capacity compared to larger bankruptcy practices, potentially affecting availability
Based solely in New York with no indication of multi-state licensing, limiting geographic reach
Website contains no client testimonials, case results, or success rate data to validate claims
No pricing transparency; "affordable" is subjective and actual fees depend on undisclosed case complexity assessment
Specialization in complex tax bankruptcy means cases with straightforward Chapter 7 may not be optimally served
best_for: New York consumers facing foreclosure who need both debt relief and home defense simultaneously
Individuals with significant IRS or state tax debt seeking resolution beyond simple payment plans
Complex debtors needing Chapter 13 bankruptcy integrated with tax settlement strategies
Consumers seeking personalized legal attention from an established local firm rather than high-volume bankruptcy mills
diagnosis: Best for New York consumers simultaneously facing debt burdens and either foreclosure or substantial tax obligations who benefit from an attorney combining accounting expertise with bankruptcy law. Primary caveat: results depend on individual financial circumstances, and clients should verify specific fees and timeline expectations during the free consultation before engaging.
services: IRS tax debt negotiation and resolution
State tax debt negotiation and resolution
Offer-in-compromise preparation and negotiation with IRS
Tax solutions through Chapter 13 bankruptcy when non-bankruptcy options unavailable
Free initial case evaluation (phone or in-person)
Long-term financial planning and debt resolution strategy
